question: Computer Graphics simplify the process of displaying:
description: CG simplifies the process of displaying pictures of any size on a computer screen, using various algorithms and techniques.

question: Computer Graphics are used in all of the following EXCEPT:
description: CG is used in entertainment, education, scientific visualization, and many more fields — but database normalization is a database design process unrelated to graphics.

question: Which of the following is NOT a type of computer graphics?
description: The main types are Raster, Vector, 3D, Interactive, and Real-Time. There is no category called Binary graphics.

question: True or False: Computer graphics deal only with 2D images.
description: False. CG deals with both 2D and 3D images — modern CG heavily focuses on 3D modelling and rendering.

question: True or False: Vector graphics lose quality when scaled.
description: False. Vector graphics keep their quality at any scale because they use mathematical equations rather than fixed pixels. This is opposite to raster graphics.

question: True or False: Computer graphics are used in virtual reality.
description: True. Virtual reality is a major application area of CG — VR relies on real-time 3D rendering for immersive experiences.

question: True or False: Animation is a single static image.
description: False. Animation is a sequence of frames where each frame is slightly different — creating the illusion of motion. A single static image is just an image.

question: Computer graphics is mainly considered as:
option_b: An art of drawing pictures using computers
description: Computer graphics is the art of drawing pictures on computer screens with the help of programming, involving computations, creation, and manipulation of data.

question: Computer graphics involve all of the following EXCEPT:
description: CG involves computations, creation, and manipulation of data — but network routing is a networking concern, not a CG concern.

question: True or False: Pixel and pel mean the same thing.
option_c: Pel is for vector, pixel for raster
option_d: Pel is a deprecated term only
description: True. Each screen point is referred to as a pixel (picture element) or pel — both terms mean the same thing.

question: What is the purpose of the deflection system in a CRT?
option_b: To direct the electron beam towards specified positions on the screen
description: The deflection system (along with the focusing system) directs the electron beam towards specified positions on the phosphor-coated screen.
